word.definition: Unctuous is a word that’s undergone change in recent years. It now often describes food that is fatty and oily, especially in a pleasing way. Formerly it was more typically applied as a formal adjective describing someone who is figuratively oily—that is, overly or insincerely flattering. Both uses can be found today.

sports_fact.text: Wilt Chamberlain averaged 50.4 points per game for the entire 1961-62 NBA season — and played all but 8 minutes of the year.

market.headline: Oil Declines Lift Stocks to Fresh Records
market.summary: Brent crude fell 19% in May, its sharpest monthly drop since 2020.

quote.text: Everything will pass, and the world will perish, but the Ninth Symphony will remain.
quote.author: Mikhail Bakunin

history.text: Pearl Hart, a female outlaw of the Old West, robs a stage coach 30 miles southeast of Globe, Arizona.
